Wrong reference: onToast should be toast
/workspace/chats/new returns 404 after Docker restart - Turbopack lazy compilation
bugs.root_cause: Turbopack lazily compiles routes on first access. After Docker restart, /workspace/chats/[thread_id] is not compiled. First access triggers 23+ second compilation but Next.js returns 404 instead of waiting. Docker volume mounts make compilation slow.
bugs.fix: Added warm-up wget to /workspace/chats/new in frontend startup command, triggered 8s after dev server starts to pre-compile the critical chat route.

bugs.error_message: AI-generated report files not appearing in docmgr 全部存档 (synced:0, skipped:0)
bugs.root_cause: Two issues: (1) Dual auth system - Gateway uses Gateway UUID for sandbox paths, but extensions docmgr sync uses extensions UUID, so sync scans wrong directory. (2) _get_thread_title queries ThreadMetaRow from Gateway SQLite via PostgreSQL session, which fails and aborts the PostgreSQL transaction, leaving subsequent queries broken.
bugs.fix: 1) Added _resolve_thread_sandbox_dir() that scans all user directories to find the actual thread sandbox dir regardless of UUID mismatch. 2) Changed _get_thread_title() to read directly from Gateway SQLite file instead of querying through the PostgreSQL session. Added from pathlib import Path to fix NameError.

bugs.error_message: Docmgr: returning from editor resets view from 全部存档 to 我的文档
bugs.root_cause: AnimatePresence mode=wait unmounts DocumentList when editor is shown, losing all local state (activeNav, currentFolder). On remount, useDocuments reinitializes with default filter { folder: currentFolder } instead of the nav-specific filter
bugs.fix: 1. Lifted activeNav and currentFolder state from DocumentList to parent DocumentManagement. 2. Added navSynced useRef + useEffect in DocumentList that restores the correct filter from activeNav on mount (e.g. { doc_type: 'file_ref' } for 全部存档)
Rewrote 17→150 lines (15 removed) | Also: ### 3.3 技术实现（嵌入 EAI Flow）; │   └── coal_data_server.py       # 煤炭数据 MCP Serve

bugs.error_message: Project members cannot see each other's documents in docmgr - strict user_id isolation prevents project-level visibility
bugs.root_cause: AIDocument.list_docs filtered strictly by user_id with no project-level visibility. Documents created in project conversations only belonged to the creating user. No project_id field on AIDocument model.
bugs.fix: 1) Added nullable project_id UUID column to AIDocument model with FK to report_projects. 2) Added DB migration ALTER TABLE + CREATE INDEX. 3) Updated list_docs to use OR(own_docs, project_docs) via ProjectMember subquery. 4) Added _detect_project_from_thread() to auto-populate project_id from thread_id. 5) Updated create, sync_thread_files, get_by_id, list_folders for project visibility. 6) Backfilled 4 existing documents with project_id.
